headline: AI Chip Bottlenecks Shift, Memory Prices Surge; Macro & Trump Policy Drive Volatility
summary: The week saw robust AI chip demand, with bottlenecks shifting to advanced packaging and manufacturing, benefiting Samsung amid TSMC capacity constraints. Memory prices, particularly HBM, are projected for significant surges. Macroeconomic data, including weaker US jobs, eased some rate hike concerns, while evolving Trump policies and geopolitical tensions contributed to market volatility. China's economy showed signs of recovery.

themes.label: US Semis / AI Chips
themes.narrative: AI chip demand remained robust, shifting bottlenecks to advanced packaging and manufacturing. TSMC accelerated 2nm and CoWoS development. Samsung gained traction for custom AI chips (Anthropic, Google) due to TSMC capacity constraints, while some chip stocks saw declines mid-week.

themes.label: Fed / Rates / Macro
themes.narrative: US labor market data showed slower gains (57k jobs added in June), easing rate hike concerns and boosting the Dow. Fed officials indicated reduced inflation risks, though AI's potential inflationary impact was noted. Supreme Court affirmed Fed independence. Oil prices reacted to US-Iran tensions.

themes.label: Memory (DRAM/HBM/NAND)
themes.narrative: Memory prices are projected to surge, with HBM contract prices forecast to increase 2.0-2.5x by 2027, driven by demand outpacing supply fivefold by 2030. UBS raised DRAM prices 32% QoQ for Q3. SK Hynix reported strong earnings, while Micron and Sandisk declined 10%. Samsung and SK Hynix announced major capex.

themes.label: Trump 2.0 / US Policy
themes.narrative: Former President Trump's financial disclosures revealed over $1.4B in crypto and stock gains, including a $250M Micron donation. Evolving policy narratives and geopolitical concerns (US-China chip policy) continued to introduce market volatility.

themes.label: China / Tariffs / Geopolitics
themes.narrative: Geopolitical concerns, particularly around US-China chip policy and Iran sanctions, weighed on sentiment. China's economy showed signs of recovery in June, driven by rebounding U.S. exports.

watchlist.name: TSMC
watchlist.note: Monitor updates on 2nm and advanced packaging capacity expansion.

watchlist.name: Samsung
watchlist.note: Track progress on custom AI chip foundry deals with Anthropic and Google.

watchlist.name: Federal Reserve
watchlist.note: Watch for upcoming inflation data and commentary on AI's economic impact.

watchlist.name: HBM Manufacturers
watchlist.note: Observe HBM pricing trends and capex plans from key players.

watchlist.name: Oil
watchlist.note: Geopolitical developments, especially regarding Iran, will influence price stability.

watchlist.name: Micron
watchlist.note: Assess performance following Trump donation news and broader memory market trends.

x_themes.topic: AI Market Downturn & Bull Case Analysis
x_themes.summary: The recent AI market downturn is attributed to Avago earnings, hawkish Fed, and bubble commentary, not fundamental demand issues like hyperscaler capex cuts or HBM oversupply. Demand for AI compute remains supply-constrained, with hyperscalers expected to increase 2026 capex forecasts.

x_themes.topic: China InP Export Controls & AXT-Coherent Supply Deal
x_themes.summary: China's discretionary InP export controls are shaping contract structures, with AXT and Sumitomo dominating InP substrate production. Coherent prepaid AXT for 6-inch InP substrate capacity, highlighting supply chain constraints and geopolitical complexities despite Coherent's domestic expansion efforts.

x_themes.topic: Custom AI Chip Proliferation & Manufacturing Bottlenecks
x_themes.summary: The rise of custom AI chips from hyperscalers like Meta and Amazon is shifting bottlenecks from chip design to physical manufacturing steps such as packaging, substrates, and PCB materials. This creates opportunities for companies like TSMC and high-end substrate suppliers.

x_themes.topic: Nvidia's Scale-Across AI Networking & Long-Haul Telecom Project
x_themes.summary: Nvidia is pushing "scale-across" networking to link distributed AI data centers, aiming to overcome power and capacity ceilings and improve GPU utilization. This strategy, potentially involving a $5B-$10B long-haul telecom project, is expected to benefit coherent transport providers like Ciena and Nokia.

Contract negotiations for 2027 HBM orders are expected to be highly favorable to the three main HBM suppliers, with Bernstein forecasting a 2.00x-2.50x price increase. This bullish outlook benefits companies like Samsung, Hynix, and Micron.

x_themes.topic: UBS Raises DRAM and NAND Price Forecasts
x_themes.summary: UBS has significantly raised its price forecasts for DRAM and NAND, expecting DRAM prices to increase 32% QoQ in Q3 and 18% QoQ in Q4, and NAND prices to rise 30% QoQ in Q3 and 12% QoQ in Q4.

x_themes.topic: Meta's NeoCloud Strategy & Tiered GPU Utilization
x_themes.summary: Meta's strategy of building a NeoCloud and renting surplus AI compute is not contradictory to its high demand, as it continues to secure significant new data center capacity. The AI compute market is evolving into tiered usage, with H100/H200 GPUs shifting to inference and next-gen systems like GB200/GB300 for frontier training.

x_themes.topic: MediaTek's Custom AI ASIC Deals and Market Position
x_themes.summary: MediaTek is actively pursuing AI accelerator chip deals, including with Meta, and is Google's primary custom ASIC client for projects like Zebrafish and Humufish. The company's custom ASIC business is projected to reach $10 billion in revenue, competing with firms like Broadcom and Qualcomm.

x_themes.topic: Samsung/Sumitomo Glass Core Substrate JV Commercialization
x_themes.summary: Sumitomo Chemical and Samsung Electro-Mechanics (SEMCO) have finalized their GlaSSEM joint venture in Korea, targeting full-scale commercialization of glass core substrates by H2 2027. This development implies a faster timeline than previously expected, benefiting equipment suppliers like LPKF.

x_themes.topic: TSMC CoPoS Mass Production & Glass-Core Substrate Evaluation
x_themes.summary: TSMC's CoPoS advanced packaging technology is expected to enter mass production in the first half of 2029. Samsung Electro-Mechanics, Toppan, and other substrate manufacturers are submitting engineering samples of glass-core substrates to TSMC for evaluation.

x_themes.topic: Meta's Accelerating AI Compute Capex & Photonics Sector Recovery
x_themes.summary: Meta's datacenter and compute investments are expected to accelerate, with "shockingly high" capex in 2027, refuting misleading narratives of Meta dropping out of the AI race. This acceleration is anticipated to drive a sharp V-shaped recovery in the photonics sector, which saw a crash based on these narratives.

HBM demand is forecast to increase fivefold by 2030, while supply is only expected to double, leading to a growing pool of unserved demand. This imbalance suggests potential for multiple expansion in the memory sector.
There are reports of significant HBM price hikes, indicating strong demand and favorable market conditions for HBM suppliers.

x_themes.topic: SK Hynix Q2 DRAM Gross Margin Forecast
x_themes.summary: Bernstein estimates that SK Hynix's DRAM gross margin will reach an exceptionally high 90.9% in the second quarter, reflecting strong profitability in the memory market.
